message:
feat(frankenpandas): add write_sql_with_options to prelude (fd90.209 / br-kyelf)

Same half-surface defect pattern as fd90.207 (read_csv_with_options):

- write_sql is in the prelude
- SqlWriteOptions is in the prelude (added by fd90.206)
- write_sql_with_options — the function that consumes SqlWriteOptions —
  was NOT in the prelude

User code building a SqlWriteOptions and calling write_sql_with_options
required an explicit `use frankenpandas::write_sql_with_options;` even
after `use frankenpandas::prelude::*`.

Fix:
- Add write_sql_with_options to crate::prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard with
  `let _ = write_sql_with_options::<rusqlite::Connection>;`

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-kusq","title":"pandas: DataFrame.pivot_table(margins=True, aggfunc='std'|'var') parity","description":"Python signature: pd.DataFrame.pivot_table(values=..., index=..., columns=..., aggfunc='std'|'var', margins=True). Current fp-frame base pivot_table supports std/var but the margins paths reject both aggfuncs. Implement sample ddof=1 std/var margin semantics, including singleton-group NaN behavior, for both pivot_table_with_margins and pivot_table_with_margins_name, with focused tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:59.846640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:14:25.012771126Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:14:25.010880739Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Keep remaining gaps concrete for future beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T12:50:31.120718911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T12:53:32.304100753Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T12:53:32.302354339Z","close_reason":"Updated conformance coverage ledger from current packet fixture inventory","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-kw5q","title":"[LOW] docs: no per-crate README.md — 11 fp-* crates on crates.io will show the workspace README (or nothing)","description":"Per-crate README audit.

**Observation.** `find crates -maxdepth 2 -name README.md` → zero matches. The workspace has 12 crates; none have a per-crate README. Users landing on crates.io for a specific fp-* crate (e.g. someone arriving at `crates.io/crates/fp-frame` from a search result) see no crate-specific documentation.

**What crates.io does without a per-crate README.** It falls back to the workspace-root README if `[package].readme = "../../README.md"` is set, OR shows nothing if the path is wrong. The workspace-root README.md is ~70 KB and workspace-oriented ("FrankenPandas: clean-room Rust reimplementation of pandas"). A user arriving at fp-frame specifically sees text about all 12 crates rather than just fp-frame.

**When it matters.** Once br-4clx ships release, each fp-* crate becomes independently discoverable on crates.io. A user searching "dataframe rust" who lands on fp-frame wants to know what fp-frame does, what it requires from other crates in the workspace (fp-columnar, fp-index, fp-runtime, fp-types), and how to use it standalone vs via the frankenpandas umbrella. Workspace README doesn't answer any of that.

**Severity.** LOW — cosmetic / discoverability. Honest calibration: release-day polish, not a blocker. br-h8a8 (crates.io metadata) partially addresses via per-crate descriptions; per-crate README is the next tier of discoverability polish.

**Fix shape.** Per-crate `crates/fp-XXX/README.md`:

```markdown
# fp-frame

DataFrame and Series with pandas-API parity and AACE index alignment.

Core crate of the [frankenpandas](https://github.com/<org>/frankenpandas)
workspace. Most users should depend on the umbrella `frankenpandas` crate
rather than fp-frame directly.

## When to use fp-frame directly

- Building a pandas-compatible Rust library that doesn't need fp-io's
  CSV/Parquet/Excel parsers.
- Embedding DataFrame/Series primitives in a higher-level crate.

## Dependencies within the workspace

- `fp-types` for Scalar, DType, NullKind.
- `fp-columnar` for Column / ValidityMask.
- `fp-index` for Index / MultiIndex.
- `fp-runtime` for RuntimePolicy / EvidenceLedger.

## Example

```rust
use fp_frame::DataFrame;
use fp_columnar::Column;
use fp_index::Index;

let df = DataFrame::from_dict(...)?;
let grouped = df.groupby(&["region"])?.sum()?;
```

See the [workspace README](https://github.com/<org>/frankenpandas) for
the full API tour and conformance status.
```

Per-crate descriptions vary — fp-io gets an "IO formats supported" section, fp-runtime documents the asupersync feature flag, etc.

Blast radius: 12 new README.md files (~500-800 lines total). Each crate's Cargo.toml needs `readme = "README.md"` (default, but set explicitly if the workspace-level `[workspace.package].readme = "README.md"` is configured — that would point every crate at the workspace README). Zero source changes. Zero CI changes.

Prerequisites:
- br-h8a8 (crates.io metadata) — this bead is the natural continuation of h8a8's per-crate description work.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — landing per-crate READMEs before first publish saves a 0.1.1 discoverability-bump.

**Related:**
- br-h8a8 (Cargo.toml description/keywords/categories) — same release-day polish wave.
- br-7cfm (rustdoc + panic contract) — per-crate README links to `docs.rs/fp-X` for the full API; rustdoc renders the full reference; README is the landing page.
- br-ddox (doctests in CI) — per-crate README examples should be doctests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:09:51.542117527Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:18.242696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:18.242696Z","close_reason":"per-crate README.md already shipped on main across all 12 crates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","docs","release","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-kyelf","title":"fd90.209: Add write_sql_with_options to prelude","description":"fd90.209: Add write_sql_with_options to the prelude.

Following the half-surface pattern of fd90.207 (where CsvReadOptions was in the prelude but read_csv_with_options was not):

- write_sql is in the prelude
- SqlWriteOptions is in the prelude (added in fd90.206)
- BUT write_sql_with_options (which consumes SqlWriteOptions) is NOT in the prelude

User code following the README that builds a SqlWriteOptions and calls write_sql_with_options(&conn, "table", &df, &opts) requires an explicit `use frankenpandas::write_sql_with_options;`.

Scope:
- Add write_sql_with_options to the prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:54:05.200126606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:55:59.117828351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:55:59.115786556Z","close_reason":"Added write_sql_with_options to the prelude. Same half-surface defect pattern as fd90.207 (read_csv_with_options) — write_sql was in the prelude, SqlWriteOptions was added in fd90.206, but the function that pairs them was missing. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard with let _ = write_sql_with_options::<rusqlite::Connection>. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-kzmxd","title":"fd90.200: Implement DataFrameResample::first/last to match README","description":"fd90.200: Add first()/last() to DataFrameResample to match README claim.

The README at line 494 enumerates Resample methods including first and last. Series Resample (impl Resample at fp-frame line 8244) has both. DataFrameResample (impl at line 8825) is missing them — exposed during fd90.199 integration test write.

Two ways to honor the README:
1. Implement DataFrameResample::first() and ::last() that delegate to per-column Resample::first()/::last() via column_as_series, mirroring the existing DataFrameResample::sum/mean/count/min/max pattern.
2. Update the README to remove first/last from the Resample row and document the asymmetry in DISCREPANCIES.md.

Option 1 is the right move — Series Resample already has the implementation, so DataFrameResample is just glue. Plus it removes the README defect at the source rather than papering over it.

Files: crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs (impl DataFrameResample around line 8825), crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (re-enable the two skipped calls in readme_window_operations).
Time estimate: 25-40min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:37:00.364880991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:46:50.966459078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:46:50.964379278Z","close_reason":"Added DataFrameResample::first() and ::last() to fp-frame (lines 8891-8907) — both delegate to Series Resample's first/last via apply_resample, matching the existing sum/mean/count/min/max/prod pattern. README line 494 listed these in the Resample row but only the .agg() string-dispatch path supported them; the direct methods were missing. Also re-enabled drs.first()? and drs.last()? in readme_window_operations test. All 1434 fp-frame lib + 34 integration + 7 frankenpandas lib + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
